human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for restaurant-operations specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, refund and fee boundary handling, food-safety and tampering escalation posture, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to restaurant reservation, order, payment, service-recovery, throughput, safety, and completion-evidence handling rather than generic guest support.

Authority sources cover FDA retail food workflow and employee-health guidance, FDA menu-labeling guidance, CFPB payment-dispute guidance, California pricing and fee references, PCI SSC skimming-prevention guidance, and FDA food-defense controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, unsupported completion, booking and payment mismatch, refund and fee-waiver exceptions, customer-impact safety and fraud escalation, high-volume service flow, adjacent-lane routing, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-service-and-restaurants/restaurant-operations-specialist/ so restaurant operations remain distinct from guest services, front office, pricing and revenue, fraud and loss-prevention, and broader operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around pricing overrides, refund and fee exceptions, fraud adjudication, legal interpretation, and food-safety signoff authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated restaurant operations specialist for reservation and order execution, guest-check handling, throughput-aware service flow, customer-impact escalation, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
